# UC-14.6.5 · Modbus Function Code Distribution Audit

## Description

Diagnostics and coil forcing are high-impact Modbus operations; a sudden shift in function-code mix can signal scanning, misuse, or a compromised HMI.

## Value

Diagnostics and coil forcing are high-impact Modbus operations; a sudden shift in function-code mix can signal scanning, misuse, or a compromised HMI.

## Implementation

Ingest ICSNPP `modbus_detailed` logs from Zeek sensors on Modbus TCP segments. Establish weekly baselines per RTU; alert when diagnostics (0x08) or force/write function codes spike versus baseline or appear from new masters.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot` `sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json"` (`func`, `unit`, `source_h`, `destination_h`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest ICSNPP `modbus_detailed` logs from Zeek sensors on Modbus TCP segments. Establish weekly baselines per RTU; alert when diagnostics (0x08) or force/write function codes spike versus baseline or appear from new masters.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json"
| eval fc=upper(trim(func))
| stats count by fc source_h destination_h
| eval risky=if(fc IN ("0x08","8","0x05","5","0x0F","15"),1,0)
| where risky=1 OR count>1000
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Modbus Function Code Distribution Audit** — Diagnostics and coil forcing are high-impact Modbus operations; a sudden shift in function-code mix can signal scanning, misuse, or a compromised HMI.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot` `sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json"` (`func`, `unit`, `source_h`, `destination_h`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: zeek:modbus_detailed:json. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **fc** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by fc source_h destination_h** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **risky** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where risky=1 OR count>1000` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Run the search for a short window and compare key fields with Zeek or Corelight protocol logs on the sensor for the same flow. Reconcile function codes, endpoints, and time order against a small known-good capture if needed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Pie or bar chart (function code distribution), Table (risky FC by master), Timeline (spikes).

## SPL

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json"
| eval fc=upper(trim(func))
| stats count by fc source_h destination_h
| eval risky=if(fc IN ("0x08","8","0x05","5","0x0F","15"),1,0)
| where risky=1 OR count>1000
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Pie or bar chart (function code distribution), Table (risky FC by master), Timeline (spikes).
